seoTitle: How to Make a Date Table in Power BI
metaDescription: Build a centralized master date table in Power BI with quick DAX parameters to support reliable time intelligence and avoid broken timeline charts.
intro: Relying on Power BI's default automatic time hierarchies can bloat your file and produce broken timeline charts when weekend or holiday dates are missing from your sales records. A dedicated master date table built with DAX gives you a foundational, unbroken timeline that supports reliable time intelligence calculations.

steps.heading: Open the Modeling ribbon
steps.body: Navigate to the Modeling tab inside Power BI Desktop to access the option for injecting a new calculated table.

steps.heading: Insert a new table row
steps.body: Create a new calculated table slot that will hold your continuous date range.

steps.heading: Script the date range with DAX
steps.body: Use quick DAX parameters to scale a continuous timeline, generating variables like Year, Month Name, and Quarter directly from the date range.

steps.heading: Expand supporting text columns
steps.body: Add additional descriptive columns as needed to support filtering and grouping in your reports.

steps.heading: Manage sorting and mark as date table
steps.body: Set custom chronological sorting properties where month names or other text fields need a specific order, then mark the table as your official system date reference.

faq.question: Why build a custom date table instead of using the default?
faq.answer: Power BI's automatic hierarchies can bloat your file size and produce broken or jagged charts when certain dates are missing from your source data.

faq.question: What DAX functions are commonly used to build a date table?
faq.answer: CALENDAR() and CALENDARAUTO() are the most common functions for generating a continuous date range.

faq.question: Do I need to mark the table as a date table?
faq.answer: Yes, marking it correctly is required for Power BI's time intelligence functions to work as expected.

faq.question: Can month names sort correctly by default?
faq.answer: No, text-based month names sort alphabetically by default, so you need to set a custom chronological sort column.
